regions.us-ports: Today Miami reports ESE 4kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting routine embarkation, disembarkation and shore excursions under light ESE 4kt winds. A Heat Advisory is in effect for Jacksonville, with heat index values up to 110°F, posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions. A Heat Advisory is in effect for Charleston and Savannah, with heat index values up to 112°F, posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions. An Extreme Heat Watch is in effect for Los Angeles and Long Beach, with temperatures up to 95°F, posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions. Over the next 24–48 hours Miami's Day 1 forecast calls for ESE 9kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 for SE 10kt with rain and less than 10% rain probability, so Port Everglades and Port Canaveral should plan maneuvering and gangway windows around the light ESE/SE 9–10kt winds. Beyond 48 hours Miami's Day 3 outlook of ESE 10kt, overcast and less than 10% rain probability indicates a persistence of ESE 10kt winds that may require attention to tender scheduling and gangway staffing for Tampa Bay and Port Everglades. Ship and terminal teams should account for the progression from ESE 4kt today to ESE/SE 9–10kt over the next three days with drizzle on Day 1 and overcast on Day 3, each noted with less than 10% rain probability, when assigning gangway staffing and tender rotations.
